title: Multiple delivery instances
principles: Multiple delivery instances describe how many eligible hits or components may occur from one activation; they are not automatically additional ammunition costs.
Fire iterations, innate pellets, multishot, burst count, and separate damage components represent different mechanisms and must not be treated as interchangeable.
Several instances may increase critical and status opportunities only when those instances can independently produce valid hits.
Pellet-style delivery may concentrate on one target or spread across several targets; instance count alone does not establish group clear.
Burst count represents sequential attacks, while innate pellet or iteration count represents simultaneous or grouped delivery unless supplied data indicates otherwise.
Separate direct and radial components may both occur from one attack but can use different status, critical, or falloff rules.
The analysis should use neutral language such as instances or damage events when the source does not explicitly identify pellets or projectiles.

single_instance: The supplied activation has one base delivery instance.
multiple_instances: The supplied activation creates several potential damage instances.
Practical value depends on independent eligibility and valid contact.
mixed_instances: The activation combines sequential, simultaneous, or component-based instances.
undetermined: The supplied fields do not establish how many independent valid instances occur.

exceptions: Do not call fire_iterations pellets unless pellet evidence is explicit.
Do not assume multishot consumes extra ammunition.
Do not multiply status chance by instance count in the generated response.
Do not assume every damage component can critically hit or apply status independently.
